ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general muffin class; no dedicated gluten-free muffin subclass exists. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0EJX covers muffins generically; no gluten-free variant code exists. Approximate match applied.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for gluten-free muffin; left null to preserve ontology honesty.

main_ingredients: gluten-free flour (rice flour or tapioca flour)
allergens: may contain tree nuts depending on flavour

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard gluten-free muffin weighs approximately 90 g, providing around 283 kcal, 42 g carbohydrate, and 23 g sugar per serving.

nutrient_source.title: Gluten Free Muffin (Any Flavour) — Singapore HPB Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for 'Gluten free muffin (any flavour)' (cr_id F11100007). All core macros and secondary nutrients are sourced from this record at confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Gluten-Free Foods — analogous estimate based on rice flour-based baked goods; reference: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for 'gluten-free muffin (any flavour)' was found in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 77 is estimated from rice flour-based baked goods (white rice flour products GI ~72–80) as documented in Atkinson et al. 2008 International Tables of GI and GL Values (Diabetes Care 31:12). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (77 × 42.0) / 100 = 32.3, rounded to 32.4. This is a high-GL food. Gluten-free flours (rice, tapioca) are well-established as higher-GI substitutes compared to wheat flour.

hypertension.key_factor: Elevated sodium (325 mg/100 g) combined with very high sugar (25.1 g/100 g) and low dietary fibre (1.96 g/100 g)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit gluten-free muffins: sodium at 325 mg/100 g contributes meaningfully to daily sodium load, and the high sugar content (25.1 g/100 g) promotes insulin-mediated sodium retention, compounding blood pressure risk with no compensating potassium or fibre benefit.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very high sugar (25.1 g/100 g), high carbohydrate (46.7 g/100 g), low dietary fibre (1.96 g/100 g), and estimated high GI (~77) from rice/tapioca flour base
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid gluten-free muffins: sugar at 25.1 g/100 g far exceeds the 20 g/100 g glycaemic ceiling, the estimated GI of ~77 indicates rapid blood glucose elevation, and the low fibre content (1.96 g/100 g) provides no meaningful glycaemic buffering — gluten-free does not mean low-GI.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High sugar (25.1 g/100 g), elevated sodium (325 mg/100 g), high energy density (314 kcal/100 g), and ultra-processed formulation
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid gluten-free muffins: the combination of high sugar (25.1 g/100 g), elevated sodium (325 mg/100 g), high fat (12.54 g/100 g), and ultra-processed ingredients contributes to dyslipidaemia, hypertriglyceridaemia, and endothelial inflammation risk, with no cardioprotective nutrients present.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Refined sugar at 25.1 g/100 g exceeds the 15 g/100 g RA threshold, with no anti-inflammatory nutrients present
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid gluten-free muffins: refined sugar at 25.1 g/100 g promotes pro-inflammatory mediators (TNF-alpha, IL-6), and the absence of omega-3 fatty acids, polyphenols, or meaningful fibre (1.96 g/100 g) provides no anti-inflammatory offset.

llm_training.natural_language_description: A gluten-free muffin is a sweet baked individual cake formulated without wheat, rye, or barley, using gluten-free flours such as rice flour, tapioca flour, or a commercial gluten-free flour blend. It is available in a wide range of flavours including blueberry, chocolate, banana, lemon, and vanilla. The texture is typically moist and dense compared to conventional wheat-based muffins, as gluten-free flours lack the protein network that provides elasticity and lift. The flavour is sweet and rich, often with a slightly gummy or crumbly crumb structure depending on the flour blend used. From a nutritional standpoint, this product is energy-dense at 314 kcal per 100 g, with a high sugar content of 25.1 g/100 g and moderate fat at 12.54 g/100 g. Carbohydrates are high at 46.7 g/100 g with low dietary fibre at 1.96 g/100 g. Sodium is elevated at 325 mg/100 g. Calcium and iron are reported as zero in the HPB source. The estimated glycemic index is high (~77), driven by the rapid-digesting nature of rice and tapioca flours, making this product unsuitable for blood glucose management. Clinically, this food is of high concern for Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, and cardiovascular disease due to its sugar, sodium, and energy density profile. It is gluten-free by formulation and suitable for individuals with coeliac disease or gluten intolerance, but it is not a health food and should be consumed only occasionally and in small portions.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a gluten-free muffin?
Can I eat gluten-free muffin if I have high blood pressure?
Is gluten-free muffin suitable for weight loss?
What flour is used in gluten-free muffins?
Gluten-free muffin vs regular muffin nutrition

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F11100007) at confidence 0.95. GI is an analogous estimate from peer-reviewed literature (Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care) based on rice flour-based baked goods, which is the closest approved-source match available. Confidence is high for macros and secondary nutrients; GI is an approved analogous estimate clearly documented.
notes: calcium_mg is reported as 0.0 and iron_mg is reported as 0.0 per the HPB Singapore Foodo database source for this product; these are confirmed zero values as reported by the approved source, not estimated. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, cholesterol_mg, potassium_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this gluten-free muffin generic entry are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database (F11100007) as of 2026-04-16. GI of 77 is an analogous estimate based on rice flour-based baked goods per Atkinson et al. 2008 International Tables of GI and GL Values (Diabetes Care 31:12); no direct GI measurement for this specific product was found in the University of Sydney GI Database. Glycemic load per serving calculated at 90 g serving size: (77 × 42.0) / 100 = 32.4. This food is not a Singaporean dish; multilingual local names (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) are not applicable and are left null. G6 multilingual compliance is satisfied as this is a Western/International generic food with no mandatory local-language requirement.
